title: Quassia
content: Quassia (or) is a plant genus in the family Simaroubaceae. Its size is disputed; some botanist s treat it as consisting of only one species, "Quassia amara" from tropical South America, while others treat it in a wide circumscription as a pantropical genus containing up to 40 species of tree s and shrub s. The genus was named after a former slave from Suriname, Graman Quassi in the eighteenth century. He discovered the medicinal properties of the bark of "Quassia amara". Distribution. Members of the genus are found in the Tropics throughout the world.
